sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच | कुतस्त्वा कश्मलमिदं विषमे समुपस्थितम् | अनार्यजुष्टमस्वर्ग्यमकीर्तिकरमर्जुन ||
transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca | kutas tvā kaśmalam idaṃ viṣame samupasthitam | anārya-juṣṭam asvargyam akīrti-karam arjuna ||
literal_meaning: The Blessed Lord said: From where has this impurity come upon you in this crisis, Arjuna? It is un-Aryan, leads not to heaven, and brings disgrace.
contextual_meaning: Krishna names Arjuna’s collapse as ‘kaśmala’—a visceral moral contamination that sabotages self-coherence. It is not just fear or sadness; it is a dissociative rupture that eclipses dharma, social esteem, and post-mortem welfare. The verse functions as a diagnostic mirror: whatever feels subjectively noble (compassion) is reframed as a shadow-impulse that will stain Arjuna’s internal and external reputation.

shadow: Fear of being seen as a butcher

psychological_state: Acute moral dissociation: values, identity, and action systems are misaligned, producing paralysis and self-loathing.
trigger_scenario: A seasoned surgeon about to operate on a loved one suddenly freezes, overwhelmed by the thought of causing harm, and contemplates walking out of the OR.
anchor_text: Stand up; shame stains the soul more than blood.
